evidence: CLI/multivol/api_server/config.py lines 11-19: six os.makedirs() calls execute at import time, creating filesystem directories as a side effect of any import. Every module that imports config (auth.py, database.py, all route files) triggers this.
CLI/multivol/api_server/config.py lines 21-22: API_TOKEN and APP_PASSWORD snapshots os.getenv() at import time. Tests cannot override via environment variable after the module is imported.

CLI/multivol/api_server/routes/scan.py line 64: runner_func = None is module-level; if any scan endpoint is hit before init_runner() is called, the route silently does nothing (guarded by 'if runner_func:') rather than raising an error.

CLI/multivol/api_server/routes/dump.py line 18: dump_tasks = {} is a module-level mutable dict accessed from the background_dump_task thread AND from GET request handlers with no threading.Lock. This is a data race.

MCP/multivol_mcp.py lines 9-13 and 30-32: API_TOKEN, BASE_URL, PORT read from env at module scope; httpx.AsyncClient constructed at module scope with the frozen token baked in — any test or reconfiguration must reload the module.

Dockerfiles/memprocfs/memprocfs_server.py lines 27-29: vmm_handle, file_cache, vmm_lock are module-level globals; file_cache is accessed without the vmm_lock in the /list endpoint (lines 187-192), creating a TOCTOU window.

strengths: memprocfs.py correctly uses a threading.Lock (sessions_lock) to guard the active_sessions dict across all mutations.
DB initialization is explicitly called once via init_db() in app.py rather than happening as a side effect of importing database.py.
Environment variable reads are centralized in a single config.py module rather than scattered across route files.
init_runner() provides an explicit injection point for the runner callback rather than relying on global import order alone.
initialization_coupling.issue_character: Issues are a mix of missing thread guards on shared mutable dicts and import-time filesystem/env side effects concentrated in config.py and the MCP server, rather than deep architectural coupling.
initialization_coupling.score_rationale: The codebase avoids the worst anti-patterns (no DB connections at import time, no circular lazy imports) and centralizes env reads. However, dump_tasks has a real data race (unlocked dict shared between a background thread and request handlers), runner_func is a deferred-init global that silently no-ops on misconfiguration, config.py performs six os.makedirs() on every import making isolated unit tests impossible without filesystem mocking, and the MCP client bakes a frozen token into a module-level singleton. These are concrete developer-experience drag points that push the score well below solid — this is a 60-range codebase on this dimension.

issues.summary: dump_tasks dict accessed from background thread and request handlers without a threading.Lock
evidence: Line 18: dump_tasks = {} defined at module scope with no associated lock.
Line 25: background_dump_task writes dump_tasks[task_id] = {'status': 'running'} in a daemon thread.
Lines 123-128: same thread writes status/output_path/error keys.
Lines 183, 197: GET request handlers read dump_tasks[task_id] concurrently with no lock.
issues.suggestion: Add a threading.Lock() beside dump_tasks and wrap all reads and writes with 'with dump_tasks_lock:'. Alternatively, use a thread-safe queue or push all state to the DB (which already has SQLite serialisation).

issues.summary: runner_func = None global silently swallows scan requests if init_runner() was never called
evidence: scan.py line 64: runner_func = None at module scope.
scan.py lines 261-262 and 709-710: 'if runner_func: runner_func(args)' — no else branch, no error raised.
app.py: run_api() calls init_runner() only when the server starts through that entry point; importing scan_bp in any other context leaves runner_func as None.
issues.suggestion: Replace the silent 'if runner_func:' guard with 'if runner_func is None: raise RuntimeError("runner not initialized")' or return an HTTP 503. Consider using a factory function or dependency-injection pattern instead of a module-level mutable.

issues.summary: config.py creates six filesystem directories as a side effect of being imported
evidence: Lines 11-19: os.makedirs(STORAGE_DIR, ...), os.makedirs(UPLOAD_FOLDER, ...) and four more are unconditional top-level statements.
Every module that does 'from multivol.api_server.config import ...' triggers these filesystem writes.
API_TOKEN and APP_PASSWORD are also frozen at import time from os.getenv(), preventing test-time override without module reload.
issues.suggestion: Move makedirs calls into an explicit ensure_dirs() function called once from app startup. Consider wrapping token reads in a lazy accessor or accepting them as arguments to run_api() to allow test overrides.

issues.summary: httpx.AsyncClient with frozen API_TOKEN constructed at MCP module scope
evidence: Lines 9-13: API_TOKEN, BASE_URL, PORT all read from env at module load.
Lines 30-32: 'client = httpx.AsyncClient(headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {API_TOKEN}"}, ...)' — client built once at import with the snapshot token.
Any reconfiguration (different token, different target URL) requires reloading the module or restarting the process.
issues.suggestion: Construct the httpx client lazily inside safe_request or during a startup hook, reading env vars at call time. At minimum, document that the token cannot be changed after import.

issues.summary: file_cache global read outside vmm_lock in /list endpoint creating a TOCTOU window
evidence: Line 29: file_cache = None at module scope.
Lines 187-192 in /list handler: 'if file_cache is not None: return jsonify(file_cache)' checked without holding vmm_lock, then 'file_cache = results' written without holding vmm_lock.
Line 172: /init endpoint resets 'file_cache = None' under vmm_lock, creating a window where one thread sees non-None cache while another thread is resetting it.
issues.suggestion: Protect all reads and writes to file_cache with vmm_lock (or a dedicated cache_lock), or use an atomic replacement pattern (assign a new object reference rather than mutating).

root_causes: Pattern of treating module-level dicts as lightweight state stores without considering thread safety implications in a multi-threaded Flask/waitress deployment.
config.py used as both a constants file and an initialization script, conflating two responsibilities and making isolated imports impossible.
